items.title: k-NN classification flow
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  query["query x"]
  train["stored training (X, y)"]
  dist["compute distances d(x, x_i)"]
  topk["select k smallest distances"]
  vote["majority vote on neighbor labels"]
  pred["predicted class y_hat"]
  query --> dist
  train --> dist
  dist --> topk --> vote --> pred
items.caption: No training fit step: at query time, compare x to all stored points, take k nearest, vote on labels.

items.title: Effect of k on decision boundary
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  smallk["small k: follows local clusters tightly"]
  largek["large k: smoother regions, more mixing"]
  bias["bias increases as k grows"]
  var["variance decreases as k grows"]
  smallk --> var
  largek --> bias
items.caption: k controls the bias–variance tradeoff for k-NN — detailed theory in Model Complexity.

items.title: Lazy learning vs eager model
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  data["labeled training data"]
  knnStore["k-NN: store data only"]
  eagerTrain["eager model: fit parameters w"]
  queryTime["new query x"]
  scan["k-NN: scan or index neighbors"]
  formula["eager: fixed predict(x; w)"]
  data --> knnStore --> scan
  data --> eagerTrain --> formula
  queryTime --> scan
  queryTime --> formula
items.caption: k-NN defers computation to inference; parametric models pay cost upfront at training.
